response: TEMPERATURE IS A MEASURE OF THE HEAT ENERGY IN A SUBSTANCE. IT IS A PHYSICAL PROPERTY THAT IS INDEPENDENT OF THE AMOUNT OF SUBSTANCE. THE TEMPERATURE OF A BODY IS THE MEASURE OF THE KINETIC ENERGY OF ITS PARTICLES. THE TEMPERATURE OF A ROOM IS A COMMONLY MEASURED VALUE IN DAY TO DAY LIFE. THE TEMPERATURE OF A BODY INCREASES WHEN IT IS EXPOSED TO A HOTTER ENVIRONMENT. THE TEMPERATURE OF A BODY DECREASES WHEN IT IS EXPOSED TO A COLDER ENVIRONMENT.
